title: Your Java → Python Bridge
subtitle: Everything you know still applies — just with less typing

sections.title: What Changes, What Stays
sections.content:
SAME CONCEPTS
OOP, exceptions, iterators, modules, lambdas, generics (typing), decorators (annotations)
DIFFERENT SYNTAX
No braces → indentation, no semicolons, def not types, self explicit
NEW SUPERPOWERS
List comprehensions, duck typing, generators, unpacking, REPL, f-strings, slicing

This course is structured as 12 focused modules. Each maps a Java concept you already know to its Python equivalent with side-by-side code, visual diagrams, and "gotcha" alerts for the tricky differences.

Your 20+ years of Java experience means you don't need to learn programming — you just need to learn Python's dialect. Think of it as learning Portuguese when you already speak Spanish.

sections.title: Your Advantage
sections.content: Most Python tutorials waste time on "what is a variable?" — you can skip straight to the patterns. Each module here assumes you understand the Java side cold and focuses on the delta.
